title: menonvillea parviflora var. aptera
side_effects: Menonvillea parviflora var. aptera can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, respiratory issues, and skin irritation. In some cases, ingesting the plant has been linked to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and vomiting. Excessive use of the plant's extracts may also lead to interactions with medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes treatments, resulting in adverse health consequences. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can cause eye and skin irritation, as well as respiratory problems.
